structured_payload_candidate.title: High Temperature Zone Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 4 alarm. If 3°C (5°F) above for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by threshold for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm for 2°C (3°F) deviation; Level 3 alarm for 3°C (5°F) deviation.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temperature above cooling setpoint by threshold.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: High-temperature alarm: 1. If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. 2. If the zone is 3°C (5°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Temperature Alarm Suppression Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Suppress zone temperature alarms after setpoint change per Section 5.1.20 and while Zone Group is in Warmup or Cooldown Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: After setpoint change or during Warmup/Cooldown Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress zone temperature alarms under specified conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Suppress zone temperature alarms as follows: 1. After zone setpoint is changed per Section 5.1.20. 2. While Zone Group is in Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Demand Limit Setpoint Adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active cooling setpoints for all zones shall be increased when a demand limit is imposed: Level 1: +0.5°C (1°F), Level 2: +1°C (2°F), Level 3: +2°C (4°F). Operator can exempt individual zones.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Increase active cooling setpoint by the specified amount for the demand-limit level. Changes are not cumulative.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The active cooling setpoints for all zones shall be increased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group. ... a. At demand-limit Level 1, increase setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand-limit Level 2, increase setpoint by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand-limit Level 3, increase setpoint by 2°C (4°F).

evidence_quote: These levels are used at the zone level (see Sections 5.3.2.6  and 5.3.2.7 ) to shed demand.

structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Decrease active heating setpoint by the specified amount for the demand-limit level. Changes are not cumulative.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Occupancy Sensor Setpoint Adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Generic Thermal Zones with occupancy switch
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When space unpopulated for 5 minutes during Occupied Mode, decrease heating setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). When populated for 1 minute, restore previous setpoints.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Occupancy switch indicates unpopulated for 5 continuous minutes during Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Decrease active heating setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F). When populated for 1 minute, restore previous values.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the switch indicates that the space has been unpopulated for 5 minutes continuously during the Occupied Mode, the active heating setpoint shall be decreased by 0.5°C (1°F) and the cooling setpoint shall be increased by 0.5°C (1°F). ... When the switch indicates that the space has been populated for 1 minute continuously, the active heating and cooling setpoints shall be restored to their previous values.

evidence_quote: 5.3.2.9.  Occupancy Sensors . For zones that have an occupancy sw itch:.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hierarchy of Set-Point Adjustments
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Setpoint adjustments shall be applied in order of priority: (a) setpoint overlap restriction, (b) absolute limits on local adjustment, (c) window switches, (d) demand limit, (e) scheduled setpoints based on Zone Group mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When multiple setpoint adjustments are active.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply adjustments in the specified priority order.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone State Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone state is Heating when heating loop output nonzero and cooling loop output zero; Cooling when cooling loop output nonzero and heating loop output zero; Deadband when neither.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine zone state based on loop outputs.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Heating. When the output of the space Heating Loop is nonzero and the output of the Cooling Loop is equal to zero. Cooling. When the output of the space Cooling Loop is nonzero and the output of the Heating Loop is equal to zero. Deadband. When not in either heating or cooling.
